name: Create Model Card
description: Create a model card from model metadata, training context, evaluation evidence, limitations, and publication caveats.

inputs.description: Model identifier, checkpoint path, or repository target.
Task, modality, or intended use context for the model.
Training or evaluation dataset identifier, card URL, or manifest.
Evaluation results file, report, benchmark reference, or metric summary.

permissions.description: Read model metadata, dataset references, evaluation summaries, license text, and existing repository metadata.

approval_points.description: Confirm model identity, intended use, evidence sources, and known gaps before drafting.
Confirm the draft sections, caveats, and unresolved evidence gaps before handoff.
Publish model card content outside the local workspace.

phases.name: Card Scope
phases.objective: Capture model identity, license, intended use, out-of-scope use, and card audience.

phases.name: Evidence Assembly
phases.objective: Collect dataset lineage, training context, evaluation metrics, limitations, and missing evidence.

phases.name: Draft Card
phases.objective: Draft card metadata and sections for model details, intended use, training data, metrics, limitations, and caveats.

phases.name: Handoff
phases.objective: Prepare final README content, patch notes, publication decision, and evidence-backed verifier summary.

required_outputs.description: Model identity, purpose, license, intended use, audience, and card scope.
Model claims mapped to datasets, metrics, reports, repository metadata, or explicit gaps.
Evaluation metrics with task, dataset or split, value, and source reference.
Draft model card sections and metadata.

artifacts.description: README.md model card draft with metadata.
Model card front matter or repository metadata block.
Evidence and gap table used to support card claims.

verifiers.description: Model identity, intended use, license, and compatibility context are documented.
Training or evaluation dataset source, revision, split mapping, and fingerprint are recorded when available.
Evaluation metrics include task, dataset or split, value, and source reference.
Model card includes intended use, limitations, datasets, training parameters, and evaluation results.
Final model card claims cite evidence table entries, metric summaries, repository metadata, or explicit gaps.
